title: dryopteris rhodolepis
intro: Dryopteris rhodolepis, also known as autumn fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia and Japan, characterized by its deciduous fronds with a distinctive rhodolepis leaflet arrangement. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris rhodolepis are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which provide numerous health benefits such as antioxidant, antiviral, and antiproliferative effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried powder or extract, and can be administered orally or used as a topical application for wound healing and skin care.
